releaseAudit.scope: Full clean-room audit: all mocks rebuilt from scratch, every source file recompiled fresh, all 21 examples recompiled fresh, README table cross-checked against disk file-by-file, full git history re-scanned for secrets, all config.h files re-verified placeholder-only, LICENSE/library.properties checked, no stray/backup files, no leftover TODO/debug markers.
releaseAudit.foundThisPass: Docs said the IoT Central dashboard shows 'synced' after an ack -- real screenshots from this session's hardware testing show it actually says 'Pending' -> 'Accepted'. Fixed in README.md, AzureIoT.h, and AzureIoT.cpp to match the real UI wording exactly.

verifiedGood: src/sha256.c + src/b64url.c + src/sas_token.c -- crypto core: RFC/NIST vectors, ASan/UBSan clean, byte-identical to independent Python cross-check
src/AzureIoT.cpp flush()/publishText() payload buffer construction -- adversarial long-key test confirms no overflow
git history (all commits, all file types) -- no leaked WiFi/Azure credentials found
src/azure_root_ca.cpp -- verbatim copy from Azure IoT C SDK, valid cert chain
config.h files across all 20 examples -- placeholder-only, no real secrets
src/AzureIoT.cpp onBoolProperty/reportBoolProperty MQTT twin topics -- cross-checked against official Microsoft docs + 2 independent third-party MQTT-direct implementations
src/AzureIoT.cpp extractJsonBool/extractJsonNumber + twin GET response parsing -- functionally tested against realistic Azure twin JSON (5 scenarios)
All 21 examples (00-20) -- compile clean (zero warnings) against a mock Arduino core + AzureIoT API

src/platform.h platformWatchdogEnable/Pet -- register/API usage cross-checked against 3+ independent sources per platform (AVR Freaks, megaTinyCore/DxCore reference docs, arduino-esp32 3.x migration notes); syntax-checked against stubbed real macro/struct shapes
src/AzureIoT.cpp reportBoolProperty() retry buffer -- functionally tested via single-TU inclusion of the real .cpp, 6 scenarios under ASan/UBSan

src/AzureIoT.cpp twin $version extraction -- verified against 6+ authoritative real Azure twin JSON examples (Microsoft Q&A, Azure IoT SDK for Java docs, Azure Samples); confirmed no collision with $lastUpdatedVersion in $metadata blocks
Full-codebase re-audit (library + dps_client.cpp + all 21 examples + git history) -- clean except the one buffer-margin bug listed below

Model-id feature (content-type fix + writable-property model-id declaration) -- fully diffed against last audit baseline, adversarial worst-case test (127-char all-colon model ID) confirms no overflow under ASan/UBSan, all 6 example config.h/.ino changes checked for consistency
bugsFoundAndFixed: ackBoolProperty()/reportBoolProperty()/requestFullTwin() topic buffers undersized for unsigned long's full range -- silently truncated $rid after request-id 100 (reproduced directly, not just reasoned about). Fixed: proper sizing + truncation checks on all three.

17_ClapToToggleLed was level-triggered with a cooldown, not edge-triggered -- sustained loud noise would retoggle repeatedly instead of being treated as one event. Fixed with rising-edge detection + cooldown for decay-tail debounce.
18_AutoNightLight/19_ComfortAlarm had no hysteresis -- real flicker risk at the threshold boundary, especially with DHT11's +/-2degC accuracy. Fixed with dual (low/high) thresholds.

19_ComfortAlarm (pre-audit, caught during design not a later find) originally overloaded 'buzzerOn' as both a writable mute-control and telemetry -- same name, two meanings. Split into 'muted' property + 'buzzerOn' telemetry.

refreshSasTokenIfNeeded()'s resourceUri buffer undersized for the true worst-case hub-hostname + device-ID length (200 needed, 192 available) -- found via -Wformat-truncation=2, not a routine build flag. Fixed: real margin + truncation check.

Design flaw caught BEFORE shipping (not a bug in committed code): a naive 'pet watchdog once per loop()' design would have conflicted with connectWiFi()'s multi-minute Wi-Fi-reconnect retry tiers. Fixed in design: pet inside that retry loop specifically, not just at the top of loop().

ensureMqttConnected()'s username buffer had only 4 bytes of real margin against the same worst-case field lengths as resourceUri -- not actually broken today, but fragile and the last snprintf without a truncation check. Fixed: check added for consistency/future-proofing.

Telemetry topic missing content-type/content-encoding declaration -- Azure IoT Hub accepted the message (so 'last data received' updated), but IoT Central couldn't reliably parse/display it. Found via a real user's hardware testing. Fixed: $.ct=application%2Fjson&$.ce=utf-8 appended to the topic.

onBoolProperty()/reportBoolProperty() never declared the device's model ID during DPS registration or MQTT connect -- first real hardware test of writable properties showed the dashboard stuck 'pending', LED never responding. Per Microsoft's own IoT Plug and Play conventions, this is required for writable-property delivery (though not for plain telemetry). Fixed: optional modelId parameter threaded through begin() to both the DPS payload and the MQTT username. NOT YET CONFIRMED on hardware as resolving the 'stuck pending' symptom specifically -- implemented and verified correct against the documented spec.

azureiot_url_encode()'s call site in the new model-id code had no truncation check, unlike every other buffer operation in the file -- safe today (real margin confirmed via adversarial worst-case test) but inconsistent. Fixed: check added for consistency.

CRITICAL: mqttMessageCallback()'s isDesiredPatch strncmp used length 38 against a 37-character string literal, including the literal's own NUL terminator in the comparison -- meant live desired-property PATCH notifications could NEVER match, for any real topic, ever (confirmed via real hardware: 'reset picks up pending changes, but LED never reacts without a reset'). The twin-GET-on-connect path used a separate, correctly-sized check and was unaffected, which is why it looked like it was working. Fixed: 38 -> 37. Root cause of why this slipped through: the existing property-parsing test only tested JSON parsing, never the topic-matching/routing logic -- added test_topic_matching.cpp (not committed, same reasons as the retry-buffer test) using the real captured topic string, verified it fails against the pre-fix code.
